name: Open-gate proteasome extended C. elegans lifespan with fecundity cost
summary: Direct intrinsic proteasome hyperactivation improved C. elegans stress resistance and lifespan but exposed a reproductive tradeoff.
statement: C. elegans engineered with an endogenous open-gate 20S proteasome had enhanced proteasomal activity, increased lifespan, and resistance to oxidative and proteotoxic stress, but also showed significantly decreased fecundity.
population_or_model: Genome-edited C. elegans expressing an endogenous alpha3 open-gate proteasome mutation
time_horizon: Adult worm lifespan, stress resistance, and fecundity assays
quantitative_note: The source reports significant lifespan extension and substantial oxidative/proteotoxic stress resistance, alongside a significant fecundity decrease.
caveats: This is a genetic worm model, not a pharmacologic mammalian intervention.
The fecundity cost is a material safety and tradeoff signal.
Open-gate proteasome biology may not translate cleanly to selective therapeutic activation.
